description_long: This object is a double-ended weapon resembling a spear or a double-edged dagger, featuring pointed blades at both ends. The central shaft is cylindrical and appears to be ribbed or grooved for grip, with symmetrical decorative elements near the transition to each blade. Both blades are leaf-shaped, tapering to sharp points, and the entire object is rendered in a dark, metallic finish. The design is sleek and utilitarian, with no visible ornamentation or color variation. The object is likely constructed from metal, giving it a hard, durable appearance suitable for combat or ceremonial use. Its proportions suggest it is intended to be wielded with two hands, offering reach and versatility.
description: A double-ended, dark metallic weapon with pointed leaf-shaped blades and a ribbed central grip. The design is symmetrical, sleek, and utilitarian.
description_view_0: A narrow, side-on view showing the thin profile of both blades and the shaft.
description_view_1: A frontal view highlighting the full width of the leaf-shaped blades and the ribbed handle.
description_view_2: Another narrow, side-on view, identical to view 0, showing the object's slim profile.
description_view_3: A frontal view similar to view 1, displaying the symmetrical blades and central grip.
